title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
summary: Jordan Urbs builds a video-editing SaaS application using only Claude Fable 5 and screenshots, completing it in one hour without specifications or wireframes. He identifies practical tradeoffs: Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be unavailable post-June 22, recommending developers evaluate Fable 5 against Opus and paid-per-use alternatives before making adoption decisions.
writeup: Jordan Urbs builds a full video editing application called Redline in a single one-shot prompt, using Claude Fable 5 through Claude Code with only SaaS landing page screenshots as input. The video walks through the build process, integration with local APIs, and a comparison against Claude Opus. Key takeaways: Fable 5 produces a working app from screenshots alone, but carries a higher token burn rate than Opus, and the plan access is set to end June 22.
